items.title: Use a set for repeated membership tests
items.avoid: `x in some_long_list` inside a hot loop.
items.prefer: Convert the list to a set once and test against it.
items.why: List membership is O(n) per test, set membership is O(1) — the speedup is dramatic on big inputs.
items.example:
```python
stop = set(stopwords)
filtered = [w for w in tokens if w not in stop]
```

items.title: Use set algebra for allow/deny logic
items.why: Set operations are concise, readable and fast.

items.title: Order-preserving dedupe when you need it
items.why: Preserves insertion order while still O(n).

items.title: Reach for frozenset when you need hashability
items.avoid: Trying to cache results keyed by a `set` of features.
items.prefer: Key the cache with a `frozenset` instead.
items.why: Plain sets are mutable and unhashable; frozensets are immutable and hashable.
